[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 江淮異人錄 - Title meaning 'Records of Strange People from the Huai and Yangtze River Regions,' where '異人' refers to extraordinary or supernatural individuals.
  • 宋 吳淑撰 - Indicates that the text was compiled by Wu Shu during the Song Dynasty.
  • 卷上 - Means 'Volume One,' indicating the first section of the work.
  • 唐寧王 - Refers to the Prince of Ning from the Tang Dynasty, a title that requires historical context.
  • 花姑 - A female character, likely a supernatural or legendary figure, whose name may need cultural explanation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 江淮 - Refers to the Huai and Yangtze River regions, historically significant areas in China.
  • 宋 - The Song Dynasty (960–1279), a period of significant cultural and literary development.
  • 唐 - The Tang Dynasty (618–907), a golden age of Chinese poetry and literature.
  • 寧王 - A title for a prince or noble, historically associated with the Tang Dynasty.
  • 異人 - Refers to extraordinary or supernatural individuals, often found in folklore and literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 江淮異人錄 - The title of the work.
  • 宋 - The Song Dynasty.
  • 吳淑 - The author of the work.
  • 唐 - The Tang Dynasty.
  • 寧王 - A title for a prince or noble.
  • 花姑 - A female character.
  • 沈汾 - A proper name.
  • 聂师道 - A proper name.
  • 李梦符 - A proper name.
  • 李胜 - A proper name.
  • 潘扆 - A proper name.
  • 陈允升 - A proper name.
  • 陈曙 - A proper name.
  • 司马郊 - A proper name.
  • 刘同圭 - A proper name.
  • 史公镐 - A proper name.
  • 董绍颜 - A proper name.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 卷下 - Indicates a section or chapter of a larger work, often used in classical texts to denote a division.
  • 耿先生 - Refers to a person named 'Geng' (耿), likely a title or honorific for a respected individual.
  • 张训妻 - Refers to the wife of Zhang Xun (张训), indicating a specific person within a family context.
  • 张标 - Refers to Zhang Biao (张标), another individual mentioned in the text.
  • 于大 - Refers to Yu Da (于大), a person's name or title.
  • 江处士 - Refers to Jiang Chushi (江处士), a term often used to denote a reclusive scholar or hermit.
  • 钱处士 - Refers to Qian Chushi (钱处士), another reclusive scholar or hermit.
  • 润州处士 - Refers to a reclusive scholar or hermit from Runzhou (润州), a place name.
  • 建康异人 - Refers to an unusual or extraordinary person from Jiankang (建康), a place name.
  • 洪州书生 - Refers to a student or scholar from Hongzhou (洪州), a place name.
  • 杭州野翁 - Refers to an elderly man or hermit from Hangzhou (杭州), a place name.
  • 糁潭渔者 - Refers to a fisherman from Santan (糁潭), a place name.
  • 宣州军士 - Refers to a soldier from Xuanchou (宣州), a place name.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐宁王 - Refers to Prince Ning of the Tang Dynasty, a historical figure.
  • 宁王善畵马 - Indicates that Prince Ning was skilled in painting horses, a common theme in Chinese art.
  • 花蕚楼壁画《六马滚尘图》 - Refers to a mural in the Huayue Building depicting six horses rolling in dust, a specific artwork.
  • 明皇最爱玉面花騘 - Indicates that Emperor Minghuang (唐明皇) loved a white horse with a jade-like face, a specific horse.
  • 花姑 - Refers to a person named 'Hua Gu' (花姑), possibly a title or nickname.
  • 宋单父 - Refers to Song Danfu (宋单父), a historical figure known for his artistic skills.
  • 牡丹变易千种 - Indicates that Song Danfu could transform peonies into thousands of varieties, a reference to his artistic or horticultural skills.
  • 明皇召至骊山 - Indicates that Emperor Minghuang summoned Song Danfu to Lishan, a historical event.
  • 沈汾 - Refers to Shen Fen (沈汾), a historical figure.
  • 唐末 - Refers to the late Tang Dynasty period.
  • 侍御 - Refers to an official title, possibly a low-ranking government official.
  • 退居乐道 - Indicates that Shen Fen retired and lived a life of virtue and pleasure.
  • 家有二妾 - Refers to Shen Fen having two concubines.
  • 若死，尔能哭我乎 - A question posed by Shen Fen to his concubines about their willingness to cry at his death.
  • 君但升榻而坐 - A command given by Shen Fen to his concubines to sit on the bed.
  • 左右拥袂而哭 - Describes the concubines crying with their sleeves held together.
  • 汾已卒矣 - Indicates that Shen Fen had already died.

Proper Nouns

  • 唐宁王 - Prince Ning of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 明皇 - Emperor Minghuang (唐明皇) of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 花姑 - A person named 'Hua Gu' (花姑), possibly a title or nickname.
  • 宋单父 - Song Danfu (宋单父), a historical figure.
  • 沈汾 - Shen Fen (沈汾), a historical figure.
  • 花蕚楼 - Huayue Building, a specific location.
  • 骊山 - Lishan, a specific location.
  • 润州 - Runzhou, a specific location.
  • 建康 - Jiankang, a specific location.
  • 洪州 - Hongzhou, a specific location.
  • 杭州 - Hangzhou, a specific location.
  • 糁潭 - Santan, a specific location.
  • 宣州 - Xuanchou, a specific location.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少好道 - Young and fond of the Dao; indicates early interest in Daoism.
  • 取啖之 - To take and eat; describes the act of trying to eat impure food to help a sick person.
  • 吾已受道法科教 - I have already received Daoist methods and teachings; indicates acceptance of Daoist practices.
  • 缒之出城 - To lower by rope out of the city; describes the method of exiting the city under siege.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 聂师道 - A Daoist from the late Tang Dynasty, known for his early interest in Daoism.
  • 歙州 - A state in present-day Anhui Province during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 于涛 - The governor of Shezhou during the late Tang Dynasty, who had a brother who was a Daoist.
  • 吴朝 - The Wu dynasty, established by Yang Xingmi, controlling parts of Jiangsu and Anhui during the Five Dynasties period.
  • 裴枢 - A government official who later became the governor of Shezhou during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 宣州田頵、池州陶雅 - Generals during the Five Dynasties period who led military campaigns against Shezhou.

Proper Nouns

  • 聂师道 - A Daoist from the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 于涛 - The governor of Shezhou during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 裴枢 - A government official who later became the governor of Shezhou during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 田頵 - A general during the Five Dynasties period.
  • 陶雅 - A general during the Five Dynasties period.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 誓約已定 - The oath and agreement were already established, indicating a formal commitment.
  • 復遣還城中 - Sent back to the city again, suggesting a return to a place of safety or authority.
  • 復見二將 - Met the two generals again, indicating a reunion after a separation.
  • 無不可，唯給事命 - Nothing is impossible, only follow your orders, showing obedience and flexibility.
  • 持此將安用邪 - What use is this to you? Expressing a rhetorical question about the value of taking something.
  • 引於曲室 - Led to a secluded room, indicating a private or hidden space.
  • 盡取金帛與之 - Gave them all the gold and silk, showing generosity.
  • 從某處出 - Leave from a certain place, indicating a specific exit point.
  • 勿犯先生 - Do not harm the master, showing respect and a command not to harm someone.
  • 感先生至仁之心 - Grateful for the master's utmost kindness, expressing deep gratitude.
  • 卒於廣陵 - Died in Guangling, indicating the place of death.
  • 見師道 - Met Master Shidao, indicating a personal encounter.
  • 何以至此 - Why are you here? Expressing curiosity about someone's current situation.
  • 朝廷遣我醮南岳 - The court sent me to perform rituals at Nanyue, indicating a religious or ceremonial mission.
  • 方知師道卒矣 - Only then did they realize that Master Shidao had died, indicating a delayed realization.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 二將 - Refers to two generals, likely military leaders.
  • 師道 - Refers to Master Shidao, a respected figure, possibly a Taoist priest or scholar.
  • 吳太祖 - Refers to the founder of the Wu kingdom, Sun Quan, who established the Eastern Wu state.
  • 廣陵 - Refers to Guangling, a city in present-day Jiangsu Province.
  • 紫極宮 - Refers to the Purple Extreme Palace, a Taoist temple or shrine.
  • 龍虎山 - Refers to Longhu Mountain, a famous Taoist mountain in Jiangxi Province.
  • 設醮 - Refers to performing Taoist rituals or ceremonies.
  • 羣盜 - Refers to a group of bandits or robbers.
  • 南岳 - Refers to Mount Heng in Hunan Province, one of the Five Great Mountains of China.
  • 湖湘 - Refers to the region of Hunan and Hubei provinces.

Proper Nouns

  • 二將 - The two generals.
  • 師道 - Master Shidao.
  • 吳太祖 - The Wu founder, Sun Quan.
  • 廣陵 - Guangling.
  • 紫極宮 - The Purple Extreme Palace.
  • 龍虎山 - Longhu Mountain.
  • 南岳 - Mount Heng.
  • 湖湘 - The region of Hunan and Hubei.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少入道 - Literally means 'entered the Dao at a young age,' but in this context, it likely refers to becoming a Daoist practitioner early in life.
  • 风貎和雅 - Describes a person as having a gentle and elegant appearance.
  • 善属文 - Means 'skilled in writing' or 'proficient in composing literature.'
  • 年二十余卒 - Indicates that the person died in their early twenties.
  • 初，绍元既病剧 - Refers to the beginning of the narrative, where it is stated that Zhao Yuan was already seriously ill.
  • 有四鹤集于绍元所处屋上 - Describes four cranes gathering on the roof of the place where Zhao Yuan lived.
  • 及其卒，人咸见五鹤冲天而去 - Indicates that when Zhao Yuan died, people saw five cranes flying away into the sky.
  • 以放荡自恣 - Describes a person who behaves freely and unrestrainedly.
  • 四时常揷花 - Means 'always wearing flowers in his hair all year round.'
  • 遍歴城中酒肆 - Describes the person visiting all the wine shops in the city.
  • 好事者多召之与饮 - Indicates that many well-intentioned people invited him to drink.
  • 或令为歌词 - Means 'sometimes asked him to compose lyrics.'
  • 应声为之 - Describes the person composing lyrics immediately upon request.
  • 初不经心，而各有意趣 - Means 'though done without much thought, each piece had its own charm.'
  • 以其狂妄惑众，将罪之 - Indicates that someone was going to punish him for his reckless behavior and influence on others.
  • 梦符于狱中献词十余首 - Describes the person composing over ten poems while in prison.
  • 揷花饮酒无妨事 - Means 'wearing flowers and drinking alcohol do not cause trouble.'
  • 樵唱渔歌不碍时 - Means 'singing while chopping wood and fishing do not interfere with time.'
  • 昨夜不归 - Indicates that the person did not return home the previous night.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 聂师道侄孙绍元 - Refers to Zhao Yuan, the nephew of Nie Shidao, a historical figure.
  • 李梦符 - Refers to Li Mengfu, a historical figure known for his free-spirited lifestyle.
  • 常游洪州市井中 - Describes Li Mengfu's frequent visits to the marketplaces of Hongzhou.
  • 年可二十余 - Indicates that Li Mengfu was in his early twenties.
  • 短小而洁白，美秀如玉人 - Describes Li Mengfu as short, fair-skinned, and beautiful, like a jade person.
  • 以放荡自恣 - Describes Li Mengfu's unrestrained and free-spirited behavior.
  • 四时常揷花 - Indicates that Li Mengfu always wore flowers in his hair, a common practice among scholars and artists.
  • 遍歴城中酒肆 - Describes Li Mengfu visiting all the wine shops in the city.
  • 好事者多召之与饮 - Indicates that many well-intentioned people invited him to drink.
  • 或令为歌词 - Means 'sometimes asked him to compose lyrics,' a common activity for poets and musicians.
  • 应声为之 - Describes the person composing lyrics immediately upon request.
  • 初不经心，而各有意趣 - Means 'though done without much thought, each piece had its own charm,' indicating the person's natural talent.
  • 以其狂妄惑众，将罪之 - Indicates that Zhong Fu was going to punish Li Mengfu for his reckless behavior and influence on others.
  • 梦符于狱中献词十余首 - Describes Li Mengfu composing over ten poems while in prison.
  • 揷花饮酒无妨事 - Means 'wearing flowers and drinking alcohol do not cause trouble,' a line from one of Li Mengfu's poems.
  • 樵唱渔歌不碍时 - Means 'singing while chopping wood and fishing do not interfere with time,' another line from one of Li Mengfu's poems.
  • 桂州刺史李琼遣使至洪州 - Refers to Li Qiong, the governor of Guilin, sending an envoy to Hongzhou.
  • 言梦符乃其弟也 - Indicates that the envoy said Li Mengfu was his brother.
  • 请遣之 - Means 'asked to release him.'
  • 锺令求于市中旅舍 - Indicates that Zhong Fu ordered a search for Li Mengfu in the city's inns.

Proper Nouns

  • 聂师道 - Refers to Nie Shidao, a historical figure.
  • 绍元 - Refers to Zhao Yuan, the nephew of Nie Shidao.
  • 李梦符 - Refers to Li Mengfu, a historical figure known for his free-spirited lifestyle.
  • 洪州 - Refers to Hongzhou, a city in China.
  • 桂州刺史李琼 - Refers to Li Qiong, the governor of Guilin.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 因爾不知所終 - Indicates that someone's fate or whereabouts are unknown.
  • 案《郡閣雅言》 - Refers to a specific text or record, *The Elegant Words of the Prefectural Hall*.
  • 李夢符，不知何許人 - Indicates that the person's origin or background is unknown.
  • 梁開平初 - Refers to the early years of the Kai Ping era during the Later Liang dynasty.
  • 日與布衣飲酒，狂吟放逸 - Describes daily activities involving drinking with commoners and unrestrained poetic expression.
  • 嘗以釣竿懸一魚，向市肆踏漁父引賣其詞 - A vivid description of a method of selling poems, using a fish on a fishing rod as a metaphorical tool.
  • 好事者爭買 - Indicates that people interested in literature or poetry were eager to purchase the poems.
  • 其詞有千余首傳於江表 - Suggests that a large number of poems were widely circulated in the southern regions.
  • 每把氷入水，及出，身上氣如蒸 - A description of a phenomenon where the person's body emits steam when ice is touched.
  • 附錄于此 - Suggests that the information is being appended or added to a larger work.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梁開平初 - Refers to the early years of the Kai Ping era (907-923) during the Later Liang dynasty, a period of political transition in Chinese history.
  • 布衣 - Refers to commoners or ordinary people, often used in contrast to the nobility or officials.
  • 江表 - Refers to the southern regions of China, particularly the areas south of the Yangtze River.
  • 漁父引賣其詞 - The practice of selling poetry or literature is a cultural reference to the literary and artistic traditions of the time.

Proper Nouns

  • 《郡閣雅言》 - The title of a specific text or record.
  • 李夢符 - The name of the person whose life and works are being described.
  • 梁開平初 - The early years of the Kai Ping era during the Later Liang dynasty.
  • 洪州 - The name of the city (modern-day Nanchang, Jiangxi Province).
  • 江表 - The southern regions of China.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 书生 - Refers to a scholar or a person of letters, often used to describe someone who is well-educated but not necessarily engaged in practical affairs.
  • 洪州西山 - Refers to the western mountains of Hongzhou (modern-day Nanchang, Jiangxi Province).
  • 处士 - Refers to a recluse or a person of learning who does not seek official position.
  • 雪夜共饮 - Describes a gathering during a snowy night for drinking and conversation.
  • 星子 - Refers to Xingzi County, located in Jiangxi Province.
  • 携书而至 - Means 'carried the books and arrived,' describing the action of bringing something back.
  • 星子距西山凡三百余里也 - Indicates the distance between Xingzi and the western mountains, which is approximately 300 miles.
  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest or monk.
  • 劲势犹动 - Means 'the force is still moving,' suggesting that the knife appears to be freshly inserted.
  • 改心礼胜 - Means 'changed his heart and treated Li Sheng with respect,' indicating a change in attitude.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 洪州西山 - A specific location in Jiangxi Province, known for its scenic beauty and cultural significance.
  • 处士卢齐及 - A reference to Lu Qi, a scholar or recluse, indicating a person of high moral character.
  • 星子 - A specific location in Jiangxi Province, known for its historical and cultural importance.
  • 道士 - A Taoist priest, often associated with spiritual and mystical practices.
  • 匕首 - A small dagger, often used in ancient times for both practical and ceremonial purposes.

Proper Nouns

  • 李胜 - The name of the protagonist, a scholar.
  • 洪州西山 - The western mountains of Hongzhou.
  • 处士卢齐及 - The name of a scholar or recluse.
  • 星子 - Xingzi County.
  • 道士 - A Taoist priest.
  • 李秀才 - A reference to Li Sheng, indicating his status as a scholar.
  • 匕首 - A small dagger.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少居于和州 - Means 'lived in Hezhou when young,' indicating the setting and time frame of the story.
  • 樵采鸡笼山以供养其亲 - Means 'he collected firewood from Jiling Mountain to support his parents,' describing the protagonist's filial piety.
  • 泊舟秦淮口 - Means 'moored his boat at the mouth of Qinhuai River,' indicating the location of the event.
  • 有一老父求同载过江 - Means 'there was an old man who asked to be taken across the river together,' introducing the key character.
  • 扆敬其老，许之 - Means 'Pan Yi respected the old man and agreed,' showing the protagonist's kindness and respect.
  • 大雪 - Refers to heavy snowfall, setting the scene.
  • 市酒与同载者饮 - Means 'bought wine to drink with the old man,' indicating the social interaction.
  • 酒已尽，扆甚恨其少，不得醉 - Means 'the wine ran out, and Pan Yi regretted having too little to get drunk,' expressing the protagonist's disappointment.
  • 吾亦有酒 - Means 'I also have wine,' indicating the old man's possession of wine.
  • 觧巾，于髻中取一小葫芦子倾之，极饮不竭 - Means 'he untied his headband, took a small gourd from his hair, and poured it out, and the wine never ran out,' describing the magical wine.
  • 及岸，谓扆曰 - Means 'when they reached the shore, the old man said to Pan Yi,' indicating the transition to the next part of the story.
  • 子事亲孝，复有道气，可教也 - Means 'you are filial and have a natural affinity for the Dao, you can be taught,' praising the protagonist and offering instruction.
  • 授以道术 - Means 'taught him Daoist techniques,' indicating the transfer of knowledge.
  • 所为绝异 - Means 'his actions were extraordinary,' highlighting the protagonist's transformation.
  • 世号曰「潘仙人」 - Means 'the world called him 'Pan the Immortal,' indicating the protagonist's new status.
  • 见池沼中落叶甚多 - Means 'saw many fallen leaves in the pond,' setting the scene for the next trick.
  • 令以物漉之，取置之于地，随叶大小，皆为鱼 - Means 'he had the leaves scooped out and placed on the ground, and they turned into fish according to their size,' describing the magical transformation.
  • 更弃于水，叶复如故 - Means 'when he threw them back into the water, the leaves returned to their original state,' indicating the reversibility of the trick.
  • 蒯毫者，请扆为术，以娱坐宾 - Means 'Kuai Mao asked Pan Yi to perform a trick to entertain the guests,' introducing the next audience.
  • 顾见门前有鐡店，请其碪以为戏 - Means 'Pan Yi saw a blacksmith shop in front of the house and asked for their hammer to use as a prop,' indicating the protagonist's resourcefulness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 潘扆 - The main character, a historical figure known for his Daoist abilities.
  • 大理评事潘鹏 - Pan Yi's father, a historical figure.
  • 和州 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location in ancient China.
  • 鸡笼山 - A mountain name, likely a specific location in ancient China.
  • 秦淮口 - The mouth of the Qinhuai River, a significant location in ancient China.
  • 潘仙人 - A title given to Pan Yi, indicating his status as an immortal.
  • 道术 - Refers to Daoist techniques or practices.
  • 蒯毫 - A historical figure who witnessed Pan Yi's tricks.
  • 鐡店 - A blacksmith shop, indicating a common setting in ancient China.

Proper Nouns

  • 潘扆 - The main character.
  • 大理评事潘鹏 - Pan Yi's father.
  • 和州 - A place name.
  • 鸡笼山 - A mountain name.
  • 秦淮口 - The mouth of the Qinhuai River.
  • 潘仙人 - A title given to Pan Yi.
  • 道术 - Daoist techniques.
  • 蒯毫 - A historical figure.
  • 鐡店 - A blacksmith shop.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 既至 - Means 'after arriving' or 'once he had arrived.'
  • 細細切之至盡 - Describes the act of cutting something very finely until it is completely consumed or used up.
  • 假人物，不可壊也 - A phrase that suggests the borrowed object is not to be damaged or destroyed.
  • 合聚之，碪复如故 - Describes the process of reassembling the object, which then returns to its original state.
  • 袖中出一幅旧方巾 - Refers to taking out an old square cloth from one's sleeve.
  • 勿轻此 - Advises not to underestimate or take lightly the significance of the object.
  • 非人有急，不可从余假之 - Indicates that the object should only be lent out in times of urgent need.
  • 他人固不能得也 - Suggests that others cannot obtain or replicate the object.
  • 能背本诵所未尝见书 - Refers to the ability to recite books by heart without having seen them before.
  • 卷而封之，置之于前 - Describes the action of rolling up a book, sealing it, and placing it in front of oneself.
  • 首举一字，则诵之终卷 - Indicates that by starting with a single word, the person can recite the entire book.
  • 其间点注涂乙 - Refers to the annotations, comments, and corrections within the text.
  • 悉能知之 - Means that the person is fully aware of all the details.
  • 此类，亦不复尽记 - Suggests that while the person can perform these feats, they are not always remembered or recounted in full.
  • 后亦以病卒 - Indicates that the person eventually died due to illness.
  • 案马令、陆游《南唐书》俱有扆传 - Refers to the fact that both Ma Ling and Lu You's *History of Southern Tang* include a biography of Pan Yi.
  • 往来江淮间，自称野客 - Describes Pan Yi traveling between the Yangtze and Huai rivers, referring to himself as a 'wild guest.'
  • 尝依海州刺史郑匡国 - Indicates that Pan Yi once relied on or stayed with the governor of Haizhou, Zheng Kuangguo.
  • 不甚见礼 - Suggests that Zheng Kuangguo did not treat Pan Yi with much respect.
  • 行笥中有二锡丸 - Refers to two tin balls found in Pan Yi's traveling case.
  • 光如白虹 - Describes the tin balls as shining like a white rainbow.
  • 人触之，身首异处 - Suggests that anyone who touches the tin balls will have their body and head severed.
  • 其所为类剑客事 - Indicates that Pan Yi's actions are similar to those of a swordsmen.
  • 后匡国知其术，表荐于烈祖 - Suggests that after Zheng Kuangguo learned of Pan Yi's abilities, he recommended him to the founding emperor of Southern Tang.
  • 召居紫极宫，数年卒 - Indicates that Pan Yi was summoned to reside in the Purple Extreme Palace and died a few years later.
  • 俱与此所载绝异，今附录于此 - Suggests that the two Pan Yis are completely different and that this information is being added as an appendix.

Cultural and Historical Allusions
